short_description_of_the_course.label: Short Description of the Course
short_description_of_the_course.value:
In this course, students will learn about system virtualisation and containerisation techniques as the foundation of cloud computing. 
Furthermore, students will acquire a working knowledge of key cloud technologies and services (such as hypervisors, container runtimes and orchestrators, and cloud infrastructure and platform services) and related system designs (such as for load balancing, partitioning, and replication) for building scalable and fault-tolerant distributed systems. As part of this, students will develop the ability to critically evaluate available deployment options in the ever-changing cloud service landscape in the context of application-specific performance, dependability, security/privacy, and efficiency requirements. 
Finally, students will gain a critical understanding of the growing environmental footprint of cloud computing as well as techniques for assessing and reducing the greenhouse gas emissions associated with cloud infrastructure and applications.

requirements_of_entry.label: Requirements of Entry
value: Networks and Operating Systems Essentials 2 (or equivalent)
Introduction to Object Oriented Programming 2 (or equivalent)
Operating Systems (H) is recommended but not required

co_requisites.label: Co-requisites
co_requisites.value: Big Data: Systems, Programming, and Management (H) (or equivalent) is recommended but not required

timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.label: Timetable and length and frequency of teaching sessions
timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.value: Two one-hour lectures and one one-hour computer laboratory session per week

course_aims.label: Course Aims
course_aims.value: This course aims to provide students with an integrated understanding and practical experiences of designing, operating, and evaluating cloud systems against given performance, dependability, security/privacy, efficiency, and sustainability requirements.

intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.label: Intended Learning Outcomes of Course

description_of_summative_assessment.label: Description of Summative Assessment
description_of_summative_assessment.value:
Two coursework elements (each worth 25%; practically embracing concepts covered in the course) and a final written examination (a written exam, 50%; focusing on the relevant theory). 

AE1 assesses ILOs 1 and 2 and focuses on benchmarking various virtual resources in the context of given application requirements, requiring students to develop their own measurement scripts that run benchmarking tools on configured cloud resources. For AE1, students are asked to work, reflect, and submit in small teams (of 3-5 students), to provide a more comprehensive learning experience. 

AE2 assesses ILOs 4 and 5 and focuses on the application of theoretical concepts on scalable and sustainable cloud design, deployment, and operation. This will evaluate students' understanding of core principles and their ability to apply them to realistic problem-solving scenarios. 

The final written exam assesses ILOs 1 to 5 (with an emphasis on ILO 3) using a combination of long-form answers and multiple-choice questions with brief justifications. The written exam is an individual assessment.

formative_assessment_feedback.label: Formative Assessment & Feedback
formative_assessment_feedback.value:
The students will be asked to give short presentations (around 5 minutes, with or without slides, ungraded) to receive formative feedback from peers and tutors in the lab sessions. The purpose of these presentations is to collect friendly feedback on the first steps, preliminary results, and further plans at a point in time when constructive suggestions can still be efficiently taken into account. 

Feedback will further be provided for each lecture chapter through a combination of ungraded short MCQ-style quizzes (around 5 questions) and ungraded in-lecture group discussion exercises (5-15 minutes).
